practices: The course skills are based on the three modes of communication: Interpretive, Interpersonal, and Presentational. The Interpretive mode is assessed through Multiple-Choice questions, with Comprehend Text accounting for 20-30%, Make Connections for 30-40%, Interpret Text for 30-40%, and Make Meanings for 10-15% of the multiple-choice section. The Interpersonal mode is assessed through the Email Reply and Conversation free-response questions. The Presentational mode is assessed through the Argumentative Essay and Cultural Comparison free-response questions. Across all modes, students must demonstrate cultural understanding and apply appropriate language skills.

keyTopics: Interpret promotional materials and letters
Analyze cultural topics such as multiculturalism and family values
Engage in an interpersonal conversation about family or community
Write an email reply to an inquiry about a community event
Deliver a short cultural presentation on a French-speaking community
Explore rites of passage and holidays in francophone cultures

units.unitTitle: The Influence of Language and Culture on Identity
keyTopics: Interpret literary texts, articles, and audio reports
Analyze themes of language and identity, nationalism, and patriotism
Write an argumentative essay based on print and audio sources
Perform a cultural comparison on how art reflects public identity
Understand the impact of new media and technology on identity
Explore contributions to World Artistic Heritage
Analyze literary texts and promotional materials related to art
Discuss ideals of beauty and aesthetics in francophone cultures
Engage in a conversation about art or a museum visit
Write an email reply related to an art event or organization
Perform a cultural comparison on the role of art in different communities
Explore performing arts, music, and literature

units.unitTitle: How Science and Technology Affect Our Lives
keyTopics: Interpret articles, charts, interviews, and presentations on technology
Analyze ethical questions and discoveries/inventions in science
Write a full argumentative essay integrating three sources
Perform a cultural comparison on the social consequences of technology
Understand and follow audio/visual instructions
Explore topics like future technologies and current research

units.unitTitle: Factors That Impact the Quality of Life
keyTopics: Interpret literary texts, interviews, and conversations about quality of life
Analyze cultural topics like education, professions, and human rights
Engage in a full interpersonal conversation task
Write a full email reply task
Perform a full cultural comparison task on quality of life
Discuss nutrition, food safety, and housing
Synthesize information from articles, charts, audio reports, and presentations
Analyze global challenges like environmental issues, health, and economic issues
Write a comprehensive argumentative essay synthesizing three sources
Perform a comprehensive cultural comparison on a societal challenge
Discuss the social impact of technology and ethical questions
Explore themes of peace and war

examFormat.multipleChoice: 65 questions, 95 minutes, 50% of score. Part A consists of 30 questions on print texts in 40 minutes. Part B consists of 35 questions on combined print and audio texts in 55 minutes.
examFormat.freeResponse: 4 questions, 88 minutes, 50% of score. Includes an Email Reply (15 minutes), an Argumentative Essay (55 minutes), a Conversation (5 turns, 20 seconds each), and a Cultural Comparison (2-minute presentation).

antiPatterns: The course de-emphasizes over-reliance on grammatical accuracy at the expense of communication. The instructional focus should be on language function in communicative tasks, not the examination of complex, isolated grammatical paradigms. For the exam, students should avoid simply summarizing sources in the argumentative essay and instead integrate them to support an argument. In spoken tasks, students should not overuse memorized stock phrases or write out full presentations to be read aloud; instead, they should speak spontaneously using key words and ideas.
